title: Proof SDK: Collaborative Editor with AI Agent Bridge
description: A guided tour of the Proof SDK — an open-source collaborative markdown editor with provenance tracking, real-time collaboration, and an HTTP bridge for AI agents.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of collaborative editing concepts (CRDTs, Yjs)

steps.title: Welcome to Proof SDK
steps.description: Proof SDK is an open-source collaborative markdown editor with provenance tracking and an AI agent bridge. The project is organized as a monorepo with npm workspaces: reusable SDK packages live in `packages/`, the editor and agent runtime in `src/`, the Express server in `server/`, and documentation in `docs/`. Let's explore each layer.

steps.title: SDK Packages (packages/)
steps.description:
The `packages/` directory contains five private npm workspace packages that form the reusable SDK core:

• **@proof/core** (doc-core) — Core document types, marks system, and provenance sidecar format
• **@proof/editor** (doc-editor) — Editor runtime with Milkdown plugins for suggestions, comments, and agent cursors
• **@proof/server** (doc-server) — Express routers for documents, shares, agents, and the collaboration bridge
• **@proof/sqlite** (doc-store-sqlite) — SQLite-backed document storage adapters
• **@proof/agent-bridge** (agent-bridge) — Typed HTTP client and route schemas for AI agent integration

Each package has a clean `src/index.ts` barrel export and can be independently consumed.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over each package directory to see its contents

steps.title: Editor Runtime (src/editor/)
steps.description:
The editor is built on Milkdown (a ProseMirror-based markdown editor) with 15+ custom plugins. Key areas:

• **plugins/** — Comments, suggestions, marks sync, agent cursors, heatmap decorations, mermaid diagrams, and more
• **schema/** — ProseMirror schema extensions for proof marks, code blocks, and frontmatter
• **index.ts** — Main editor initialization and configuration
• **actor.ts** — Actor pattern for document mutations
• **batch-executor.ts** — Batch operation execution

The plugin system is the heart of the collaborative editing experience.

interactiveActions.description: Click plugins/marks.ts to see the core marks plugin

steps.title: Agent System (src/agent/)
steps.description:
The agent system lets AI assistants collaborate on documents through an HTTP bridge. Key components:

• **orchestrator.ts** — Agent orchestration and task coordination
• **http-bridge-client.ts** — HTTP client for agent-to-server communication
• **proposals.ts** — Proposal generation for agent-suggested edits
• **session-manager.ts** — Agent session lifecycle management
• **skills/** & **tools/** — Pluggable agent capabilities
• **prompts/** — Prompt templates for agent interactions

Agents can read document state, add comments, make suggestions, and apply full rewrites — all through the bridge API.

interactiveActions.description: Click orchestrator.ts to see how agents coordinate edits

steps.title: Bridge Protocol (src/bridge/)
steps.description:
The bridge layer defines how agents communicate with documents. It sits between the agent system and the server:

• **bridge-routes.ts** — Route definitions and execution interfaces
• **bridge-executor.ts** — Executes bridge operations (comments, suggestions, rewrites)
• **collab-client.ts** — WebSocket client for real-time collaboration sync
• **marks-preservation.ts** — Ensures marks survive across bridge operations
• **share-client.ts** — Share token and permission management

This is the contract layer documented in `AGENT_CONTRACT.md`.

steps.title: Express Server (server/)
steps.description:
The server provides the HTTP API and real-time collaboration backend. Major areas:

• **db.ts** — SQLite database layer (document CRUD, marks, events, idempotency)
• **collab.ts** — Yjs collaboration runtime and WebSocket sync
• **document-engine.ts** — Document mutation execution
• **agent-routes.ts** & **agent-edit-v2.ts** — Agent API endpoints and edit operations
• **bridge.ts** & **bridge-auth-policy.ts** — Bridge router and authentication
• **discovery-routes.ts** — `.well-known/agent.json` service discovery

The server runs on Express 5 with better-sqlite3 for persistence and WebSocket rooms for real-time sync.

interactiveActions.description: Click db.ts to see the SQLite storage layer

steps.title: Data Formats & UI (src/formats/, src/ui/)
steps.description:
Supporting layers round out the runtime:

**src/formats/** — Core data structures:
• **marks.ts** — Marks serialization (comments, suggestions, rewrites)
• **provenance-sidecar.ts** — Provenance metadata tracking authorship
• **remark-proof-marks.ts** — Remark plugin for proof marks in markdown

**src/ui/** — Custom web-component UI:
• Agent presence indicators and cursors
• Context menus, review menus, and theme picker
• Welcome card and name prompt dialogs

steps.title: Tests & Documentation
steps.description:
The project has strong test coverage and documentation:

**src/tests/** — 130+ integration tests covering agent edits, bridge operations, share permissions, collab race conditions, and more. Tests run via `tsx` for direct TypeScript execution.

**docs/** — Architecture decision records, the provenance spec (v2), agent API docs, and the Proof editor skill definition.

**AGENT_CONTRACT.md** — The public API contract for agent bridge integration.

**apps/proof-example/** — Example application showing agent HTTP bridge usage.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over src/tests to see the breadth of test coverage

steps.title: Architecture Recap
steps.description:
Here's the full picture:

🔵 **packages/** — Reusable SDK packages (@proof/core, editor, server, sqlite, agent-bridge)
🟢 **src/editor/** — Milkdown-based editor with 15+ plugins
🟣 **src/agent/** — AI agent orchestration and session management
🩷 **src/bridge/** — Bridge protocol connecting agents to documents
🔴 **server/** — Express API, SQLite storage, and Yjs collaboration runtime
🟡 **src/formats/** — Marks and provenance data structures

To get started: run `npm run dev` for the Vite dev server and `npm run serve` for the Express backend. Check `AGENT_CONTRACT.md` for the bridge API, and `docs/agent-docs.md` for agent integration details.
